description:
Goal
Compiler2 should do runtime work only for values that are demanded by an executable boundary. Exact callable use is semantic evidence, not representation obligation. The compiler must preserve the exact callable surfaces it already discovers and derive ABI lanes, callable entries, native callable boundaries, and closure materialization only from settled runtime demand.

Why this replaces the narrow bug
The old symptom was src/compiler2/drive_test.rs:2781 failing in compiler2_abi_ready_does_not_publish_unused_callable_constructors: ABI-ready programs publish callable_entries for closure bodies even when no call or escape demands them. That is not a local ABI bug. It is a modeling bug: compiler2 discovers exact callable use, throws it away into runtime_callable_values, then later recovers callable obligations from types. Type recovery is inherently too broad and will keep producing unused callable constructors, extra callable entries, wrong closure surfaces, and ABI leaks.

Core distinction
Semantic fact: which callable surface is actually used? The source of truth is CallSiteSummary.targets[*].surface_inputs plus exact escape/opaque evidence.
Representation fact: which first-class callable values must exist at runtime? This is derived later from runtime demand.

Correct model
Add a root/executable-scoped runtime demand plan. ActivationKey.input remains semantic. Semantic activation inputs do not imply runtime ABI lanes. Runtime parameter layout is derived from demand after semantic closure is sealed.

Demand vocabulary
RuntimeDemand = Ignore | Value | TupleFields(Vec<RuntimeDemand>) | Callable(CallableDemand).
CallableDemand preserves exact demand as resolved surfaces, opaque, and escape. Resolved(surface_inputs) means a direct callable invocation needs that callable surface. Opaque means the callable reaches a boundary that can call it with unknown shape. Escape means a first-class callable value must survive as data.

Representation derivation
Only after runtime demand settles, derive ABI-ready callable entries, native callable boundaries, backend operands, and codegen closure materialization. Direct-only resolved callable demand may pass executable identity plus demanded captures without allocating/publishing a first-class closure. Opaque or escaping callable demand requires first-class callable materialization and a callable entry boundary.

Removal target
Remove callable-specific type recovery paths: runtime_callable_values, runtime_callable_boundary_values, note_callable_arg_uses, ClosureBoundaryUse::CallableArg, closure_value_needs_runtime_boundary, resolve_materialized_runtime_callable_boundary_activations, ABI-ready callable entry derivation from arbitrary callable value types, native callable boundary selection from capture types, and native callable identity collection from all callable-producing steps.

Signal
Existing quicksort tuple-field delivery must remain green. compiler2_abi_ready_does_not_publish_unused_callable_constructors must pass for the right reason: no demand, no callable entry. Add demand telemetry so tests can assert semantic work versus representation work without fixed dump strings.

Strategy
Work data-model-up. First model demand, then compute the demand closure, then derive ABI layouts and callable materialization from that single plan, then remove backend/native/interpreter recovery logic that was compensating for missing demand. Each child ticket is one green commit and closes after its checks pass.

Design reference
See proposal.html for the full worked examples, but tickets below carry the required data structures, algorithms, removals, and checks so the plan survives without relying on local context.
title: Compiler2 runtime value demand: derive ABI and callable work from demand
